destination.name: Mosel
destination.hero_image_alt: Mosel wine country, steep slate Riesling slopes above the river loop at Bremm
destination.blurb: Germany's most dramatic wine landscape, where Riesling clings to blue and red slate slopes up to 70 degrees steep above the looping Mosel, Saar and Ruwer rivers.
destination.tagline: Riesling on slate, from feather-light Kabinett to noble Trockenbeerenauslese
destination.overview: The Mosel runs from Trier to Koblenz through the world's steepest concentration of vineyards, with Riesling on blue and red Devonian slate giving wines from racy Kabinett to nobly sweet Trockenbeerenauslese and dry GG. The region folds in the Saar and Ruwer valleys and the terraced Lower Mosel.

index.title: Mosel Wine Region Guide | Cork & Curve
index.description: Explore the Mosel: Riesling estates, slate sub-regions and iconic cuvees across the Mittelmosel, Saar, Ruwer and Terrassenmosel, from J.J. Prum to Egon Muller.

vineyards.title: Mosel Vineyards and Estates | Cork & Curve
vineyards.description: Mosel Weinguter worth visiting, from J.J. Prum and Fritz Haag to Egon Muller and Maximin Grunhaus. Tasting programmes and Riesling sites for 40+ producers.

wines.title: Mosel Wines Catalog | Cork & Curve
wines.description: Browse Mosel cuvees from Wehlener Sonnenuhr and Brauneberger Juffer Riesling to Scharzhofberger Auslese. Taste profiles, food pairings and vintage scores.

tasting-rooms.title: Mosel Tasting Rooms | Cork & Curve
tasting-rooms.description: Mosel Vinotheken and cellar doors worth booking, from riverside estate tasting rooms in Wehlen and Zeltingen to Saar and Ruwer Riesling houses.

wine-bars.title: Mosel Wine Bars and Vinotheken | Cork & Curve
wine-bars.description: Riesling-focused wine bars and Vinotheken across Bernkastel-Kues, Trier and the Mosel villages pouring Kabinett, Spatlese and dry GG by the glass.

wine-restaurants.title: Mosel Wine Restaurants | Cork & Curve
wine-restaurants.description: Mosel restaurants with serious Riesling cellars, from estate kitchens to Trier and Bernkastel dining rooms that pour the region's slate-grown whites.

signature-wines.title: Iconic Mosel Wines | Cork & Curve
signature-wines.description: The bottles that define the Mosel, from Egon Muller Scharzhofberger and J.J. Prum Wehlener Sonnenuhr to Fritz Haag Brauneberger Juffer and Dr. Loosen Erdener Pralat.

signature-grapes.title: Mosel Signature Grapes | Cork & Curve
signature-grapes.description: Mosel grape varieties, led by Riesling on slate, plus Muller-Thurgau, Elbling on the Obermosel, Spatburgunder, Kerner and Weissburgunder across the region.

neighborhoods.title: Mosel Sub-Regions and Wine Villages | Cork & Curve
neighborhoods.description: The Mosel's sub-regions and villages, from the Mittelmosel around Bernkastel, Wehlen and Piesport to the Saar, Ruwer, Terrassenmosel and Obermosel.

faqs.q: What is the Mosel wine region known for?
faqs.a: The Mosel is the world's leading region for Riesling grown on steep blue and red slate slopes. Its wines range from low-alcohol off-dry Kabinett and Spatlese to nobly sweet Auslese, Beerenauslese and Trockenbeerenauslese, with a growing number of bone-dry Grosses Gewachs bottlings.

The greater Mosel covers the Mittelmosel (the heartland around Bernkastel, Wehlen, Graach, Piesport and Brauneberg), the Saar and Ruwer tributary valleys, the terraced Terrassenmosel or Lower Mosel near Winningen, and the Obermosel on the Luxembourg border where Elbling is grown.

faqs.q: How do I read a Mosel wine label?
faqs.a: Most labels combine a village and vineyard (such as Wehlener Sonnenuhr or Brauneberger Juffer) with a Pradikat ripeness level (Kabinett, Spatlese, Auslese, Beerenauslese, Trockenbeerenauslese or Eiswein). VDP estates also use a vineyard hierarchy topped by VDP.Grosse Lage, with dry wines from those sites labelled Grosses Gewachs (GG).

faqs.q: Can you visit Mosel wine estates?
faqs.a: Many estates run a Vinothek with tastings and cellar-door sales, often by appointment for the smaller family domaines. Bernkastel-Kues, Trier and the villages between have walk-in wine bars and Vinotheken, and the region hosts festivals such as the Bernkastel wine festival and Mythos Mosel.
